birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: thumb|Winter in Vermont The barred owl is distributed throughout most of the eastern United States, as well as much of southern Canada. They are found as far northeast as much of Nova Scotia (western two-thirds), New Brunswick and Sept-Îles, in much of Quebec, up to Lake Mistassini, and Ontario, up to Moosonee.{{cite book|author=Erskine, A

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: |last2=Young |title=The Red-billed Tropicbird Phaethon rubricauda in Hawaii, with notes on interspecific behavior of tropicbirds |journal=Marine Ornithology |volume=35 |year=2007 |pages=81–84 |url=https://www.researchgate.net/publication/237377707 |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20180217202830/https://www.researchgate.net/profile/Lindsay_Young/publication/237377707_The_Red-billed_Tropicbird_Phaethon_rubricauda_in_Hawaii_with_notes_on_interspecific_behavior_of_tropicbirds/links/00b7d52c271783ce39000000.pdf |archive-date=2018-02-17 }} It sometimes wanders further, including five records from Great Britain, and two from Australia: October–December 2010 on Lord Howe Island and September 2014 on Ashmore Reef. In July 2005, one was found in eastern New Brunswick, Canada, likely the same bird that later visited Seal Island and Matinicus Rock, Maine every summer from 2005 to 2021.{{cite web |title=Why does this red-billed tropicbird keep returning to Maine?

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: Some populations of blue-winged teals nest along the Atlantic Coast from New Brunswick to Pea Island, North Carolina. They migrate in flocks to winter in to the south of its breeding range
